property="instapp:owner_user_id" content="8771449052" /><meta name="description" content="1,477 likes, 45 comments - dabelle_2018 on March 17, 2026: &quot;Bauchi State is one of Nigeria&#x2019;s largest and most culturally rich regions &#x2014; home to diverse ethnic groups, sweeping savannah landscapes, and one of the country&#x2019;s greatest tourism assets, Yankari Game Reserve.

Yankari alone spans over 2,200 square kilometres, and since the 1960s, it has stood as a symbol of Nigeria&#x2019;s tourism potential &#x2014; from its wildlife to the natural warm springs, to that raw, untouched beauty you don&#x2019;t easily find elsewhere.

But beyond the destination, there&#x2019;s the journey.

Bauchi is trying &#x2014; you can see it in the new roads, the bridges rising, the infrastructure gradually taking shape. Still, the road reminds you of a familiar Nigerian reality: development often comes in phases&#x2026; sometimes slow, sometimes uneven. Yet, there is progress.

And maybe that&#x2019;s part of the story.

Standing beneath one of the first bridges still under construction in the heart of the city, I found myself documenting more than just a structure &#x2014; it felt like capturing a moment in transition&#x2026; a state becoming and the life around and under this structure.

Because tourism doesn&#x2019;t start at the destination&#x2026;
it starts from the journey.

This trip was sponsored by Swan Water Nigeria and Gala Chinchin

Because tourism doesn&#x2019;t start at the destination&#x2026;
it starts from the journey.

by Shewandersfar\ud83c\udf39 on May 23, 2026.","caption":{"text":"My Visit to the Mobee Royal Family Slave Relics Museum, Badagry\n\nDuring my visit to the Mobee Royal Family Slave Relics Museum in Badagry, I learned about the history of the trans-Atlantic slave trade and the role Badagry played as a major slave port. The museum, owned by descendants of the Mobee royal family, preserves original relics from that period, including slave chains, shackles, manacles, branding irons, and historical artifacts used during the trade.\nWalking through the museum was both educational and emotional. The guides explained how enslaved Africans were captured, traded, and transported through Badagry before being taken across the Atlantic Ocean. Seeing the original relics gave me a deeper understanding of the suffering endured by millions of people during that era.\nThe visit helped me appreciate the importance of preserving history and remembering the lessons of the past. The museum stands today as a powerful reminder of human resilience, cultural heritage, and the need to promote freedom, dignity, and human rights for all.#badagry #slavestrade #explorepage\u2728 #visitnigerianow #fypagee\u2764\ufe0f\u2764\ufe0f \n\nTour guide \u0040jexmarvel \n\nPhoto credit \u0040dabelle_2018"},"code":"DYsR40LDonV","display_uri":"https:\/\/scontent-los4-1.cdninstagram.com\/v\/t51.82787-15\/706676954_18467531467111175_8345109111641405146_n.jpg?stp=dst-jpg_e35_s640x640_tt6&_nc_cat=102&ccb=7-5&_nc_sid=18de74&efg=eyJlZmdfdGFnIjoiQ0FST1VTRUxfSVRFTS5iZXN0X2ltYWdlX3VybGdlbi5DMyJ9&_nc_ohc=1hF-YVxG-U4Q7kNvwGqMoBT&_nc_oc=AdpPN5xZ1eNDR00-kSR3G8EVlV7a4Ye5PDmZV4GyclOkwWlKYWw146NPh14Z9cuw7To&_nc_zt=23&_nc_ht=scontent-los4-1.cdninstagram.com&_nc_gid=ukQqgcJSHWKieTrd7Ye3hA&_nc_ss=7c689&oh=00_Af86LVsubiTHtOo4X9ncU5-quYroA72ftQoC4OJz_Xf10w&oe=6A2FD727","media_type":8,"product_type":"carousel_container","user":{"pk":"3193447174","username":"shewandersfar_","id":"17841403335229180"},"carousel_media_count":13,"id":"POLARIS_3903573648124840405"}},{"node":{"__typename":"XIGPolarisCarouselMedia","pk":"3902112443577067643","__isXIGPolarisMedia":"XIGPolarisCarouselMedia","accessibility_caption":"Photo by Dabelle on May 21, 2026.","caption":{"text":"Not performing. Transforming.\nThe TIV SWANGE DANCE\n\nIn this moment, the stage becomes more than a platform\u2014it becomes a bridge between the physical and the spiritual, the past and the present. Every movement carries the weight of tradition, every step echoes stories passed down through generations, and every gesture speaks a language older than words. Beneath the mask and adorned in symbols of heritage, the dancer is no longer simply an individual but a living embodiment of culture, strength, and identity.\n\nThis is not just dance; it is storytelling in motion. State","profile_pic_url":null},"like_count":1477,"like_and_view_counts_disabled":false,"comment_count":45,"commenting_disabled_for_viewer":null,"caption":{"pk":"18092984681041216","has_translation":null,"text":"Bauchi State is one of Nigeria\u2019s largest and most culturally rich regions \u2014 home to diverse ethnic groups, sweeping savannah landscapes, and one of the country\u2019s greatest tourism assets, Yankari Game Reserve.\n\nYankari alone spans over 2,200 square kilometres, and since the 1960s, it has stood as a symbol of Nigeria\u2019s tourism potential \u2014 from its wildlife to the natural warm springs, to that raw, untouched beauty you don\u2019t easily find elsewhere.\n\nBut beyond the destination, there\u2019s the journey.\n\nBauchi is trying \u2014 you can see it in the new roads, the bridges rising, the infrastructure gradually taking shape. Still, the road reminds you of a familiar Nigerian reality: development often comes in phases\u2026 sometimes slow, sometimes uneven. Yet, there is progress.\n\nAnd maybe that\u2019s part of the story.\n\nStanding beneath one of the first bridges still under construction in the heart of the city, I found myself documenting more than just a structure \u2014 it felt like capturing a moment in transition\u2026 a state becoming and the life around and under this structure.\n\nBecause tourism doesn\u2019t start at the destination\u2026\nit starts from the journey.\n\nThis trip was sponsored by Swan Water Nigeria and Gala Chinchin\n\nCc: \u0040shewandersfar_ my tour buddy, \u0040aboki_tour  our tour Guide\n\n#bauchi #tournigeria #visitnigerianow #bauchipeople #somewhereinnigeria","created_at":1773761588},"caption_is_edited":false,"carousel_media":[{"__typename":"XIGPolarisImageMedia","accessibility_caption":"Photo by Dabelle on March 17, 2026. style="line-height:18px"><time class="xdwrcjd" dateTime="2026-03-17T15:33:08.000Z" title="Mar 17, 2026">12w</time></span></div><span class="x193iq5w xeuugli x13faqbe x1vvkbs xt0psk2 x1i0vuye xvs91rp xo1l8bm x5n08af x126k92a" style="line-height:18px">Bauchi State is one of Nigeria’s largest and most culturally rich regions — home to diverse ethnic groups, sweeping savannah landscapes, and one of the country’s greatest tourism assets, Yankari Game Reserve.<br/><br/>Yankari alone spans over 2,200 square kilometres, and since the 1960s, it has stood as a symbol of Nigeria’s tourism potential — from its wildlife to the natural warm springs, to that raw, untouched beauty you don’t easily find elsewhere.<br/><br/>But beyond the destination, there’s the journey.<br/><br/>Bauchi is trying — you can see it in the new roads, the bridges rising, the infrastructure gradually taking shape. Still, the road reminds you of a familiar Nigerian reality: development often comes in phases… sometimes slow, sometimes uneven. Yet, there is progress.<br/><br/>And maybe that’s part of the story.<br/><br/>Standing beneath one of the first bridges still under construction in the heart of the city, I found myself documenting more than just a structure — it felt like capturing a moment in transition… a state becoming and the life around and under this structure.<br/><br/>Because tourism doesn’t start at the destination…<br/>it starts from the journey.<br/><br/>This trip was sponsored by Swan Water Nigeria and Gala Chinchin<br/><br/>Cc: <a class="x1i10hfl xjbqb8w x1ejq31n x18oe1m7 x1sy0etr xstzfhl x972fbf
